step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the selection of B (Platelet). While neutrophils and monocytes possess MHC class II molecules during activation, platelets maintain no detectable MHC proteins throughout their lifespan due to being devoid of a nucleus and any associated organelles containing genetic material necessary for antigen presentation. Therefore, platelets consistently represent the definitive example among all listed options illustrating complete MHC antigen absence.
